author: pk910
content:
I've been playing through the "fillup queue and let the fee spike to the hundredths range in one block"-issue I've mentioned above.
I was able to pack up to 8500 builder deposits into a single 200M gas block. As the fee adjusts only once at the end of the block,  all these deposits cost 1 wei fee (assuming the queue was empty before).
Clearing up 8500 deposits takes about 6 mins (33 blocks),  so not really an issue on that side.

The fee raises quite extreme.
With 704 entries, the fee is 1 ETH
With 1021 entries, the fee is about the total mainnet ETH supply 😄

What's a bit alarming:
With more than 3017 entries in the queue,  the fee calculation overflows the uint256 range. The fee is jittering in a unexpected way from this point onward.  I didn't find a value lower than the total ETH supply yet,  but it's still highly undefined behavior.

I think it's not a critical issue to leave the fee calculation as is, but the overflow should be fixed.
Recalculating the fee per tx instead of once per block would still be cleaner, but probably not worth the extra effort..
